game_name: Where in the World is Carmen Sandiego?

design_philosophy.core_loop: Travel to cities, interview witnesses for clues, use reference materials to deduce suspect identity and location, apprehend criminal before time limit expires
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure vs. information gathering; incomplete clues require deductive reasoning across global geography
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: International detective solving globe-spanning crimes through investigation and geographical knowledge
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Pedagogical design embedding geography education into core gameplay loop; copy-protection via mandatory reference book creates dependency on external knowledge resource; non-punitive wrong-path exploration (witnesses simply provide no information rather than failure states)

difficulty.continues_system: Time-based deadline per case; player loses if time expires before arrest
difficulty.checkpointing: Case-by-case progression with automatic sleep cycles resetting daily timer
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Difficulty scales with rank progression; higher ranks feature more potential locations and suspect complexity; player controls investigation pace and resource allocation (time spent per location)

progression.upgrade_system: Rank advancement system triggered after solving sufficient cases; rank determines case difficulty and suspect pool size
progression.unlock_mechanism: Geography quiz questions (copy-protected) gate rank promotions; final case unlock requires reaching highest rank to pursue Carmen Sandiego herself
replayability_hooks: Procedural case generation with multiple possible suspect paths
Hall of Fame ranking system for high-rank completions
Multiple versions with expanded content (Deluxe version features 3200+ clues vs. base version)

world_building_techniques: V.I.LE organization framing providing antagonist faction context
Progressive revelation of Carmen Sandiego as final boss through rank advancement
Location-specific witness dialogue hinting at thief movement and identity
Contextual crimes tied to famous stolen works and real-world geography
Brief animation sequences showing henchmen confirmation when player reaches correct location

boss_design.philosophy: Final confrontation with Carmen Sandiego serves as culmination of rank progression; differs mechanically from standard criminal cases only in dialogue (witnesses warn player to be careful) and visual threat indicators (thrown weapons, gunfire animations)
notable_bosses: Carmen Sandiego (final case; requires correct warrant and correct location selection among three options)

faction_indicators: V.I.LE. organization as criminal network with distributed henchmen across global locations
ACME Detective Agency as player faction with rank-based hierarchy
Interpol as neutral authority database for warrant issuance
danger_signals: Witness warnings at final location ('be careful' dialogue)
Animated weapon attacks (knives, gunfire) during final location investigation
Time pressure creating urgency of criminal apprehension
environmental_storytelling: Each location identified by prominent city representing country geography
Witness testimonies grounding story in specific geographic and cultural contexts
Stolen famous works establishing crime stakes and location relevance

sources_used: Wikipedia: Where in the World Is Carmen Sandiego? (1985 video game)
